ab: Manga’ay kako palicay i tisowanan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Manga’ay to.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Manga’ay kako palicay i tisowanan haw?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Na’ay! A:wa^ ko kafana’ ako.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: Cilimaw kiso i herek no lahok haw? Mangalay kako mitengil i tisowanan.
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Nga’ay to. Talaen ako kiso i loma’.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: Cilimaw kiso anocila^ hay? Mangalay kako a mitengil i tisowanan to kongko^ no Pangcah.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於阿美族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legends of the Amis.

ab: Awaay kako i loma’. Talalotok kako.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: O nikatengil ako, mataneng ko fana’ iso to rayray no Pangcah, saka mangalay kako a mitengil i tisowanan.
ch: A:我聽說您對阿美族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Amis and I would like to ask you something.

ab: Caay ka talolong ko fana’ ako, nika sa’icel hato ako a paca’of.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Manga’ay kako a misya-sing haw? O sakacaloway a mikilok i ikor.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Nga’ay to.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Pina^ to ko mihecaan iso anini^?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Siwa^ to a polo’ ira ko falo^.
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: Masakapah ho^ kiso.
en: A: You're so young.

ab: Cowa^ sa, mato’as to!
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: Nani cowa^ kiso a tayni?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Nani Fata’an kako.
en: B: I come from Matthew Saddle.

ab: O Pangcah ko fafahi^ iso haw?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是阿美族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Ami?

ab: Caay, o Piwma^ ciira.
en: B:No, she's a Puyuma.

ab: Pina^ a sasowalan ko kafana’ iso?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Mafana’ kako a somowal to sowal no Pangcah ato no Piwma^.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I speak Aramaic and Puyuma.

ab: Lipahak kako anini^ a palicay i tisowanan. Painien ho kako to pakayniay i paysin to pi’adop haw?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Hay, mararid kako a talalotok yo o kapah ho.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Aray ko pipaini iso anini^ a romi’ad.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: O so’elinay ko nipaini no mako, o nano rayray a nipaini no to’as.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Hatini aca^ ko pipalicay ako anini^. Aray!
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Hay!
en: B: You're welcome.

ab: Itoan to kako. O tatayni ho^ kako a mitengil to adihayay a kongko^ iso.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Hay, na’on hato ko rakat.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
